rulings.Case Title: ROBERT COURTNIER VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Travelers Objections To Settlement Conference Order
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for October 18, 2016, Tuesday, in Department 503, Line 2. Specially appearing Travelers Casualty and Surety Company's objections to settlement conference order are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Anyone who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: DEAN ARMSTRONG VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Defendant Gorman-Rupp Companys Motion For Relief Of Notice Of Mandatory Settlement Conference Requiring Disclosure Of Insurance Information
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for October 18, 2016, Tuesday, in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Armstrong International, Inc.'s joinder in The Gorman-Rupp Company's motion for relief of notice of mandatory settlement conference requiring disclosure of insurance information is denied. Defendant shall anticipate that this tentative ruling will be adopted and must designate one carrier representative who possesses full and final settlement authority to be present at the October 18, 2016 Mandatory Settlement Conference. Confidential disclosures are due to the court immediately following the October 18, 2016 hearing. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: DEAN ARMSTRONG VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Motion For Relief From The Courts September 29, 2016, Order Requiring Disclosure Of Insurance Information And Attendance Carrier Representatives At Msc
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for October 18, 2016, Tuesday, in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant The Gorman-Rupp Company's motion for relief from the Court's September 29, 2016, order requiring disclosure of insurance information and attendance of carrier representatives at MSC is denied. Defendant shall anticipate that this tentative ruling will be adopted and must designate one carrier representative who possesses full and final settlement authority to be present at the October 18, 2016 Mandatory Settlement Conference. Confidential disclosures are due to the court immediately following the October 18, 2016 hearing. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES ESQUIVEL VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for October 18, 2016, Tuesday, in Department 503, Line 1. Nancy T. Williams, Brayton Purcell LLP's motion to be relieved as counsel for plaintiff Martha Esquivel is granted. Counsel demonstrated she provided Ms. Esquivel with proper notice of the motion and no opposition was filed. Hearing required unless counsel informs plaintiff of this tentative ruling and she states that she does not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 9:30a.m. Brayton Purcell must bring a proposed order to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/GLW)
